name: J2EE Bad Practices: Direct Use of Sockets
description: A J2EE application directly uses sockets for communication instead of leveraging framework-provided method calls. The J2EE specification only permits socket use for legacy system communication when no higher-level protocol exists. Custom communication protocols introduce security risks.
detection_pattern: Look for STRUCTURAL nodes (J2EE application code) creating EXTERNAL nodes (raw socket connections, ServerSocket, Socket) for communication instead of using the proper STRUCTURAL mediator (framework APIs, higher-level protocol handlers, container-managed communication) that provides secure, managed communication.
